## Highlights

- **Materially less context cost per dispatch.** D35 splits the 477-line `core/process.md` by audience + phase — every cardinal except `team-lead` saves significantly. Backend Phase 4 dispatch: **-38%** process lines. QA Phase 5: **-48%**. `ai-engineer`: **-58%**. (#89)
- **Auditable token economy.** New `scripts/measure-role-context.ps1` measures framework-only context cost per cardinal; results live in [`reference/CONTEXT_COSTS.html`](https://kostiantyn-matsebora.github.io/ginee/reference/CONTEXT_COSTS.html) and a Pester test gates per-role byte ceilings + doc currency on every PR. Drift in either direction now fails CI. (#100)
- **Cleaner file organization.** `*-protocol.md` specs moved under `core/protocols/`; team-lead-only kernel summaries (GitHub integration · Triage scoring · Post-task check-in) moved to `core/process/dispatch.md` so specialists never pay for orchestration content. (#98, #99)
- **Release-checklist step** in `CLAUDE.md` — 5 steps to refresh the cost snapshot + verify gate before tagging. Catches the drift pattern that bit this very release. (#100)

## Adopter migration

Nothing to do for the runtime — D35 + the reorganisations apply on your next dispatch once the kernel reload picks up the new structure.

**Stuck with stale citations?** If your `local/*` files cite specific anchors:

- `core/process.md § Phase <N>` → `core/process/phase-<N>-<name>.md` (full mapping in [`core/MIGRATIONS/D35-process-md-load-topology.md § Anchor migration`](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D35-process-md-load-topology.md))
- `core/iteration-protocol.md` (et al.) → `core/protocols/iteration-protocol.md`

Full specs: [D35](https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/blob/main/core/MIGRATIONS/D35-process-md-load-topology.md) · [Context-costs page](https://kostiantyn-matsebora.github.io/ginee/reference/CONTEXT_COSTS.html).

**Full Changelog:** https://github.com/kostiantyn-matsebora/ginee/compare/v0.13.0...v0.14.0
